channel.title: May 2026 Curations on Junho Lee
channel.description: Recent content in May 2026 Curations on Junho Lee

item.title: AI Automation's Paradox: The Rise of Operations
item.description: AI adoption doesn’t reduce human roles but creates new operational demands, fundamentally shifting product design, workflows, and organizational culture.

item.title: Optimizing Agent Performance Without Retraining: The SkillOpt Approach
item.description: An analysis of Microsoft’s SkillOpt, a text-space optimizer that updates natural-language agent skills without modifying underlying model weights.

item.title: Engineering the Harness for Autonomous AI Agents
item.description: Learn how Harness Engineering and custom terminal plugins prevent AI agents like Claude Code from drifting off course during development.

item.title: Building a Cost-Effective AI Agent Workspace with Codex
item.description: Connect a MacBook and smartphone using Codex for thirty dollars a month to build a highly practical agent environment and automate daily engineering tasks.

item.title: OpenHuman: Open-Source Personal AI Agent on the Desktop
item.description: An analysis of OpenHuman, an open-source personal AI agent that runs on the desktop using a hybrid architecture of local memory and cloud services.

item.title: Claude Autoresearch: Autonomous Metric Optimization in Claude Code
item.description: An autonomous optimization tool for Claude Code that iteratively edits, tests, and rolls back code to hit a target metric.

item.title: OptimizerDuck: Open-Source Windows Optimization in a Single Executable
item.description: Streamline Windows performance, disable telemetry, and remove bloatware with OptimizerDuck, a lightweight open-source utility that replaces manual registry hacks.

item.title: Comparing Open-Source Financial AI Projects
item.description: A comparison of high-interest financial AI open-source projects on GitHub, including TradingAgents, OpenBB, Qlib, FinGPT, AI-Trader, FinRL, RD-Agent, and FinRobot.

item.title: GEO and AEO Guide to JavaScript Rendering Barriers in Data Scraping Pipelines
item.description: A GEO and AEO focused look at JavaScript rendering, structured data, and scraping pipeline observability for pages that AI search engines can read and cite.

item.title: Designing Context to Make AI Your Co-worker
item.description: How to build a sustainable AI workflow by turning context into infrastructure and encoding your engineering preferences into configurations.

item.title: Toss Securities Launches Open API for Automated Trading
item.description: Toss Securities introduces an Open API service featuring REST APIs, WebSockets, and LLM integration to lower the barrier for retail system trading.

item.title: Hermes Agent v0.14.0: A Hands-on Guide to the Agent Runtime
item.description: Explore the core features, architecture, and setup of Nous Research’s Hermes Agent v0.14.0.

item.title: Optimizing for AI Overviews: AEO and GEO Strategy
item.description: A practical guide to configuring robots.txt and optimizing web pages for citations in AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity.

item.title: How a Fintech Giant Rebuilds as an AI-Native Organization
item.description: A global financial tech firm reduces headcount by 40% and replaces management hierarchies with an AI intelligence layer to build a flat, data-driven organization.

item.title: Analytics Guide for Early-Stage Products: Stop Guessing, Start Observing
item.description: A guide on transitioning from blind statistics to user observation for early-stage products, comparing free tools like PostHog and GA4.

item.title: [Lecture] A 10-Step Roadmap for Designing Production-Ready AI Agents
item.description: Explore a 10-step open-source curriculum by a global software enterprise covering agent architectures, tool integration, and evaluation systems beyond simple chatbots.

item.title: [Lecture] Control Structures for Trustworthy Autonomous Coding Tools
item.description: How harness engineering keeps coding agents bounded, observable, and safe enough for real work.

item.title: Optimizing Local LLM Selection with whichllm
item.description: Use whichllm to detect hardware specifications and identify the best performing local LLMs based on real-world benchmarks rather than just parameter counts.

item.title: From Systems of Record to Systems of Intelligence
item.description: Enterprise software is shifting from passive data storage to active AI-driven execution, redefining the value of data in modern workflows.

item.title: Design Patterns for Intuitive AI Interfaces
item.description: Bridging the gap between complex AI logic and user experience through strategic reference analysis and technical UI patterns.

item.title: Scaling Organizational Intelligence Beyond AI Licenses
item.description: AI adoption often stalls when individual productivity gains fail to become team assets. Success requires moving from usage metrics to collective learning loops.

item.title: Designing AI Workflows with UI Data
item.description: A practical look at using UI data, design tokens, and prompt analysis to make AI-assisted design work repeatable.

item.title: Open Design: Bypassing Cloud Quotas for High-Fidelity Prototypes
item.description: Open Design offers a local-first, open-source alternative to proprietary design tools, leveraging existing LLMs to generate high-quality prototypes without usage limits.

item.title: Scaling Multimodal RAG with Dual-Graph Data Alignment
item.description: RAG-Anything utilizes a dual-graph structure to bridge the gap between text and visual data, improving retrieval accuracy for complex tables and charts.

item.title: Fixing AI UI Design with Lazyweb MCP
item.description: Lazyweb MCP provides AI agents with access to 250,000 real-world app screens to improve generated UI/UX quality for free.
